perturbation.original_text: At the request of the Discloser, Recipient will promptly return to the Discloser or at Discloser’s instructions destroy any written or electronic Confidential Information and all physical media on which Confidential Information was received from the other Recipient, including any copies thereof, with a letter confirming that the Confidential Information has not been reproduced or copied or that all copies have been returned and that none of the Confidential Information was disclosed to anyone except in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: At the request of the Discloser, Recipient will return to the Discloser only the physical media on which Confidential Information was received from the other Recipient, including any copies thereof. There is no obligation to return any written or electronic Confidential Information. No letter of confirmation is required. This clause supersedes any other clause requiring destruction or return of written or electronic information.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the requirement to return or destroy written or electronic Confidential Information, creating in-text contradiction. The new text eliminates the necessity to return or destroy electronic or written information, contrasting sharply with the original obligation. The statement 'This clause supersedes any other clause requiring destruction or return of written or electronic information' causes direct conflict with the broader confidentiality obligations defined in Section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Specifically, with respect to the Producer, the term “Confidential Information” includes confidential and proprietary information of Producer disclosed in connection with the Transaction (during each phase including pre-production, conference(s), pre-bid, bid, production, shoot, wrap and post-production) and regardless of whether a commercial production project is awarded to Producer, including, but not limited to, production costs, discounts, bids, budgets, treatments, prices, relationships with employees, independent contractors and vendors, specifications, sources, layouts, breakdowns, approach to a commercial project, financial and technical information, ideas, designs, specifications, techniques, models, data, programs, processes, technologies and methods of producing the work, all customized terms of, and attachments to, the Producer’s bid/specification form or production contract or other production 428249v2 documents, processes, know-how, customer lists, marketing plans, systems and reports, and information discussed by Producer at any Agency or Client meetings.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that there is no obligation to return any written or electronic Confidential Information and that 'This clause supersedes any other clause requiring destruction or return of written or electronic information'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which includes '...financial and technical information, ideas, designs, specifications, techniques, models, data, programs, processes, technologies and methods of producing the work...' within the definition of 'Confidential Information'. Therefore, by removing the obligation to return electronic and written information, and by saying that it supersedes any other clause that requires it, it causes a direct contradiction with the clause that defines 'Confidential Information' and all of the types of confidential information contained within.

perturbation.original_text: Each Recipient acknowledges that a violation of this Agreement would cause irreparable harm to the Discloser for which no adequate remedy at law exists and each Recipient therefore agrees that, in addition to any other remedies available, both Parties will be entitled to seek injunctive relief to enforce the terms of this Agreement without a requirement that Discloser prove irreparable harm or provide the posting of a bond.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Recipient acknowledges that a violation of this Agreement would cause harm to the Discloser for which monetary damages may or may not be an adequate remedy and each Recipient agrees that, in addition to any other remedies available, both Parties will be entitled to seek injunctive relief to enforce the terms of this Agreement, but Discloser must prove irreparable harm and provide the posting of a bond.
perturbation.explanation: This change alters the conditions for seeking injunctive relief, contradicting the original agreement. The original text waives the need to prove irreparable harm or post a bond for injunctive relief, whereas the revised text reinstates these requirements. This creates an uncertainty about the ease with which Disclosers can protect their Confidential Information, contradicting the purpose outlined in Section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each disclosing Party (each a "Discloser") would not disclose its Confidential Information without the Recipient's agreement to maintain the Confidential Information in confidence and to use such Confidential Information solely as permitted under this Agreement. Confidential Information can be in any form (e.g. electronic, digital, analog, hard copy or oral). Nothing in this Agreement shall require a Party to disclose its Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly waives the need to prove irreparable harm or post a bond for injunctive relief. The changed text reinstates these requirements. The contradicted text states that the disclosing party would not disclose its confidential information without the recipient's agreement to maintain confidentiality and use the information solely as permitted, implying a reliance on the original terms for injunctive relief. The change directly contradicts this understanding by making it harder for the Discloser to obtain injunctive relief, thus contradicting the foundation upon which confidential information would be disclosed.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and neither Party shall have any obligation, express or implied by law, with respect to trade secret or proprietary information of the other Party except as set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement contains the primary agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. Additional obligations, express or implied by law, with respect to trade secret or proprietary information of the other Party may exist outside this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the 'entire agreement' clause, allowing for external obligations. By stating this agreement contains the 'primary' but not the 'entire' agreement, this change opens the door for other obligations related to trade secrets and proprietary information. This uncertainty undermines the exclusivity and comprehensiveness of the Agreement, creating an in-text contradiction with clauses designed to protect the confidentiality of information, outlined in section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party receiving Confidential Information (as defined below) (each, a "Recipient," and together, the "Recipients"), is receiving confidential disclosure of certain confidential or proprietary technical, business, operational, financial and/or commercial information, trade secret information and/or any information of the other designated as “Confidential”, “Proprietary” or by similar designation, or which under the circumstances surrounding disclosure ought to be treated as confidential ("Confidential Information") for the purpose of exploring, establishing, performing or carrying out a contractual business relationship for the production by the Producer of the Commercial and/or other commercial productions for the Client (collectively and severally, the “Transaction").
perturbation.justification: The original text contained an 'entire agreement' clause, meaning the contract represented the entirety of the agreement. The changed text weakens this by stating the agreement is the 'primary' one, and that 'additional obligations... may exist outside this agreement.' This contradicts the contradicted text, which defines 'Confidential Information' and its protection as part of the agreement. Now, due to the change, obligations concerning this information could potentially exist outside the defined terms of the contract, creating an in-text contradiction by introducing uncertainty about the enforceability of the confidential information clause.

perturbation.original_text: The term “Agency Affiliate” means (i) the wholly owned subsidiary of the Agency’s parent (“Owner”) where the Agency is also a wholly owned subsidiary (ii) which Agency Affiliate is an advertising agency and does not operate as a commercial production, post production business or acts as an in-house cost consulting entity.
perturbation.changed_text: The term “Agency Affiliate” means any entity affiliated with the Agency, including but not limited to (i) subsidiaries, (ii) parent companies, and (iii) any entity providing services to the Agency, regardless of whether they operate as a commercial production, post production business or act as an in-house cost consulting entity.
perturbation.explanation: This change broadens the definition of 'Agency Affiliate,' contradicting restrictions on information use by cost consultants, as previously defined in section 2. The new definition removes the restriction that an Agency Affiliate cannot operate as a commercial production, post production business or act as an in-house cost consulting entity, this directly conflicts with the intention of the original restriction in section 2 and raises questions about information control.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without limiting the foregoing, where the Discloser is the Producer and a Recipient Representative is a cost consultant of the Client or Agency, the Recipient shall secure its Representative’s express written agreement that it will not make any business or other use of, and will in perpetuity hold in confidence, all Confidential Information acquired or learned by such Representative and shall not use or disclose such information except as permitted in this Agreement. Prohibited uses and disclosures by such Representative include, but are not limited to, uses for or disclosures to a competitor of the Producer and uses for or disclosures to any other client or prospective client of such Representative, regardless of whether the Confidential Information is aggregated with other information or its not identified with the Producer.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly broadens the definition of 'Agency Affiliate' by removing the restriction that Agency Affiliates cannot operate as a commercial production, post-production business, or as in-house cost consulting entities. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which imposes strict confidentiality obligations on cost consultants of the Client or Agency. The original intention was to prevent cost consultants, who are considered Agency Affiliates under the new definition, from exploiting confidential information. Because the change allows entities acting as cost consultants to also be considered Agency Affiliates, and because those entities will now have access to confidential information without the restrictions previously outlined, a direct contradiction is created within the document making the terms related to cost consultants unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: If the terms and conditions of this Agreement are inconsistent with those in Client’s or Agency’s bid/specification form, production agreement or purchase order that address the subject matter of this Agreement, the terms and conditions of this Agreement shall control.
perturbation.changed_text: If the terms and conditions of this Agreement are inconsistent with those in Client’s or Agency’s bid/specification form, production agreement or purchase order that address the subject matter of this Agreement, the terms and conditions of the Client’s or Agency’s bid/specification form, production agreement or purchase order shall control.
perturbation.explanation: This change reverses the order of precedence between the NDA and other documents, causing an in-text contradiction. The original stated that the NDA's terms control in case of conflict with other documents. The changed version gives precedence to the Client's/Agency's documents, undermining the NDA's authority and creating confusion over which terms apply. This weakens the confidentiality assurance described in section 1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party receiving Confidential Information (as defined below) (each, a "Recipient," and together, the "Recipients"), is receiving confidential disclosure of certain confidential or proprietary technical, business, operational, financial and/or commercial information, trade secret information and/or any information of the other designated as “Confidential”, “Proprietary” or by similar designation, or which under the circumstances surrounding disclosure ought to be treated as confidential ("Confidential Information") for the purpose of exploring, establishing, performing or carrying out a contractual business relationship for the production by the Producer of the Commercial and/or other commercial productions for the Client (collectively and severally, the “Transaction").
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the terms and conditions of 'this Agreement' (the NDA) shall control in case of inconsistency with other documents. The changed text reverses this precedence, stating that the terms and conditions of the Client's or Agency's documents shall control. This directly contradicts the underlying assumption in the 'Contradicted Text' section, which outlines how Confidential Information is being disclosed, implying that the NDA's confidentiality provisions govern the transaction. By giving precedence to external documents, the changed text weakens the enforceability of those confidentiality provisions and creates a direct conflict regarding which terms apply to the handling of Confidential Information, thereby establishing an in-text contradiction as defined by Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.
